ex.en: Don't try to fool me, I know the truth.
ex.ko: 나를 속이려 하지 마, 나는 진실을 알고 있어.

fool.en: a person who lacks good sense or judgment

ex.en: He felt like a fool after making that mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 그 실수를 하고 나서 바보가 된 기분이었다.

fool.en: behave in a silly or playful way (often 'fool around')

ex.en: Stop fooling around and finish your homework.

footballer.en: a person who plays football (soccer), especially professionally

ex.en: He dreams of becoming a professional footballer.
ex.ko: 그는 프로 축구선수가 되는 것을 꿈꾼다.

forbid.en: order someone not to do something

ex.en: Smoking is forbidden inside the building.

force.en: make someone do something they do not want to do

ex.en: They forced him to sign the paper.
ex.ko: 그들은 그가 그 서류에 서명하도록 강요했다.

ex.en: The force of the wind knocked down the tree.

force.en: an organized group of soldiers or police officers

ex.en: The police force arrived within minutes.
ex.ko: 경찰 병력이 몇 분 만에 도착했다.

forest.en: a large area of land covered with trees

ex.en: They went hiking in the forest.

forever.en: for all future time; without ever ending

ex.en: I will remember this day forever.
ex.ko: 나는 이 날을 영원히 기억할 것이다.

fork.en: a tool with points used for eating or serving food

ex.en: She ate the salad with a fork.

fork.en: a point where a road or path divides into two

ex.en: Turn left at the fork in the road.

fortunately.en: it is lucky that (used to introduce a good outcome)

ex.en: Fortunately, no one was hurt in the accident.
ex.ko: 다행히, 그 사고로 다친 사람은 없었다.

fortune.en: the good or bad things that happen to someone by chance

ex.en: She had the good fortune to meet him early in her career.
ex.ko: 그녀는 경력 초기에 그를 만나는 행운을 얻었다.

fortune.en: a very large amount of money

ex.en: He made a fortune in the software business.
ex.ko: 그는 소프트웨어 사업으로 큰 재산을 모았다.

forward.en: toward the front or in the direction someone is facing

ex.en: She leaned forward to hear better.
ex.ko: 그녀는 더 잘 들으려고 앞으로 몸을 기울였다.

forward.en: send a message or email on to another person

ex.en: Can you forward me that email?

frame.en: put something such as a plan or question into words in a particular way

ex.en: She framed her question very carefully.

frame.en: a border that surrounds a picture, window, or door

ex.en: He put the photo in a wooden frame.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사진을 나무 액자에 넣었다.

frame.en: make an innocent person appear guilty of a crime

ex.en: He claimed he was framed for the robbery.
ex.ko: 그는 자신이 그 강도 사건의 누명을 썼다고 주장했다.

frank.en: honest and direct in what you say

ex.en: To be frank, I don't think this plan will work.
ex.ko: 솔직히 말해서, 나는 이 계획이 성공할 거라고 생각하지 않아.

freedom.en: the state of being free to do or say what you want

ex.en: People fought hard for their freedom.

freeze.en: change from a liquid into ice because of cold

ex.en: The lake freezes every winter.

freeze.en: suddenly stop moving because you are frightened or shocked

ex.en: She froze when she saw the snake.
ex.ko: 그녀는 뱀을 보고 그 자리에서 얼어붙었다.

freeze.en: officially stop prices, wages, etc. from rising or changing

ex.en: The company decided to freeze all salaries this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 모든 급여를 동결하기로 결정했다.

fresh.en: recently made, picked, or produced; not old

ex.en: We bought some fresh vegetables at the market.
ex.ko: 우리는 시장에서 신선한 채소를 좀 샀다.

fresh.en: clean and pleasant, or new and different

ex.en: Let's go outside and get some fresh air.
ex.ko: 밖에 나가서 상쾌한 공기를 좀 마시자.

fridge.en: an appliance that keeps food cold

ex.en: Put the milk back in the fridge.

fried.en: cooked in hot oil or fat

ex.en: I love fried chicken.
Their friendship has lasted for over twenty years.
The loud noise frightened the baby.
ex.ko: 그 큰 소음이 아기를 놀라게 했다.

ex.en: She was frightened of the dark as a child.
It was a frightening experience for everyone.

fry.en: cook food in hot oil or fat

ex.en: She fried some eggs for breakfast.
Make sure the door is fully closed.

function.en: the purpose or job of something

ex.en: The main function of this app is to track your spending.
ex.ko: 이 앱의 주요 기능은 지출을 추적하는 것이다.

function.en: work or operate in the way it is supposed to

ex.en: The heart stopped functioning properly.

function.en: (mathematics) a relationship in which each input has exactly one output

ex.en: This is a graph of a simple linear function.

furniture.en: movable objects such as tables, chairs, and beds used in a room

ex.en: They bought new furniture for the living room.

further.en: to a greater degree or extent

ex.en: We need to discuss this further.
ex.ko: 우리는 이것에 대해 더 논의할 필요가 있다.

ex.en: Please contact us for further information.
ex.ko: 추가 정보를 원하시면 저희에게 연락해 주세요.

gallery.en: a room or building where works of art are shown

ex.en: We spent the afternoon at the art gallery.

garage.en: a building or part of a building where a car is kept

ex.en: He parked the car in the garage.

garage.en: a place where cars are repaired

ex.en: I took my car to the garage to get it fixed.

garlic.en: a small strong-smelling bulb used to flavor food

ex.en: Add two cloves of garlic to the sauce.

gas.en: a fuel in the form of gas, used for cooking or heating

ex.en: We cook on a gas stove.

gas.en: a substance like air that is neither solid nor liquid

ex.en: Oxygen is a gas that we need to breathe.
ex.ko: 산소는 우리가 숨쉬는 데 필요한 기체이다.

gas.en: (American English) fuel used to make a car engine run

ex.en: We need to stop and get some gas.
ex.ko: 우리는 멈춰서 휘발유를 좀 넣어야 한다.

gate.en: a barrier like a door in a wall or fence

ex.en: Please close the gate when you leave.

gate.en: the place at an airport where passengers get on a plane

ex.en: Our flight leaves from gate 12.

gather.en: collect things together from different places

ex.en: She gathered her books before leaving.
A crowd gathered outside the stadium.

gather.en: conclude something from information you have

ex.en: I gather you're not happy with the decision.
ex.ko: 네가 그 결정에 만족하지 않는다는 걸 알겠어.

gender.en: the state of being male or female

ex.en: The form asks for your name, age, and gender.
ex.ko: 그 양식은 이름, 나이, 성별을 묻는다.

generation.en: all the people born and living around the same time

ex.en: Three generations live together in this house.

gentle.en: kind, calm, and not rough in how you treat people

ex.en: She has a very gentle personality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 온화한 성격을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: There was a gentle breeze on the beach.

given.en: already stated, decided, or agreed on

ex.en: At any given time, only ten people can enter.
ex.ko: 정해진 어느 시점이든 열 명만 들어갈 수 있다.

globe.en: a model of the Earth shaped like a ball

ex.en: There was a globe on the teacher's desk.
People all around the globe watched the game.
ex.ko: 전 세계 사람들이 그 경기를 시청했다.

glove.en: a covering for the hand, worn to keep it warm or protected

ex.en: Wear gloves, it's cold outside.

golden.en: having the color or shine of gold

ex.en: The sunset turned the sky golden.
This is a golden opportunity to travel abroad.

golf.cue: 작은 공을 채로 쳐서 구멍에 넣는 운동
golf.en: a sport in which players hit a small ball into holes with clubs

ex.en: He plays golf every Sunday morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매주 일요일 아침 골프를 친다.

ex.en: Her new boyfriend is very good-looking.

government.en: the group of people who officially control a country

ex.en: The government announced new tax rules.

grace.en: a smooth, beautiful, and elegant quality in movement or manner

ex.en: The dancer moved across the stage with grace.
ex.ko: 그 무용수는 우아하게 무대를 가로질러 움직였다.

grace.en: extra time allowed before a deadline or penalty

ex.en: The bank gave us a 30-day grace period.

gradually.en: slowly, over a period of time

ex.en: The weather gradually got warmer.

graduate.en: a person who has completed a course of study at a school or university

ex.en: She is a graduate of Seoul National University.

graduate.en: complete a course of study and receive a degree

ex.en: He graduated from high school last year.

gram.en: a metric unit of weight, one thousandth of a kilogram

ex.en: The recipe needs 200 grams of flour.

grandchild.en: the child of your son or daughter

ex.en: They have three grandchildren.
My granddad tells great stories.
ex.ko: 우리 할아버지는 이야기를 정말 재미있게 하신다.

granddaughter.en: the daughter of your son or daughter

ex.en: Her granddaughter visits her every weekend.
ex.ko: 그녀의 손녀는 매주 주말마다 그녀를 찾아온다.

grandson.en: the son of your son or daughter

ex.en: His grandson looks just like him.

granny.en: an informal word for grandmother, or an old woman

ex.en: We're visiting Granny this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 이번 주말에 할머니 댁에 방문한다.

ex.en: I'm grateful for all your help.

greatly.en: very much; to a large degree

ex.en: Her health has greatly improved.

greedy.en: wanting much more money or things than you need

ex.en: He was too greedy to share his profits.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 탐욕스러워서 이익을 나누지 않았다.

greedy.en: wanting to eat much more food than you need

ex.en: Don't be greedy, leave some cake for others.
ex.ko: 욕심부리지 말고 다른 사람들 몫으로 케이크를 좀 남겨.

grilled.en: cooked over direct heat, such as on a grill

ex.en: We had grilled chicken for dinner.

grocery store.en: a store that sells food and household items

ex.en: I need to stop by the grocery store on my way home.
ex.ko: 집에 가는 길에 식료품점에 들러야 해.

guard.en: protect a person or place from danger

ex.en: Two dogs guard the house at night.
ex.ko: 밤에는 개 두 마리가 집을 지킨다.

guard.en: a person whose job is to protect a place or person

ex.en: The guard checked everyone's ID at the door.

guidebook.en: a book that gives useful information about a place for travelers

ex.en: She bought a guidebook before her trip to Rome.
ex.ko: 그녀는 로마 여행 전에 가이드북을 샀다.

ex.en: The police officer carries a gun.
These shoes are half-price this week.

ham.en: meat from a pig's leg, often smoked

ex.en: She made a ham sandwich for lunch.

handbag.en: a small bag used to carry money and personal items, especially by women

ex.en: She left her handbag on the chair.

handicapped.en: having a physical or mental condition that limits movement or activity

ex.en: The building has a ramp for handicapped visitors.
ex.ko: 그 건물에는 장애가 있는 방문객을 위한 경사로가 있다.

handle.en: deal with a situation, problem, or task

ex.en: She handled the complaint very calmly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 불만을 아주 침착하게 처리했다.

handle.en: the part of an object that you hold to use or move it

ex.en: The handle on this bag is broken.

happily.en: in a happy or joyful way

ex.en: The children played happily in the garden.

happily.en: fortunately (used at the start of a sentence)

ex.en: Happily, the storm passed without causing damage.
ex.ko: 다행히도, 그 폭풍은 피해 없이 지나갔다.

ex.en: Money can't buy happiness.
I hardly know him.

harm.en: damage or injury caused to someone or something

ex.en: The storm caused a lot of harm to the crops.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍은 작물에 큰 피해를 입혔다.

harm.en: cause injury or damage to someone or something

ex.en: The chemicals can harm the environment.
ex.ko: 그 화학 물질은 환경에 해를 끼칠 수 있다.

harmful.en: causing or able to cause harm

ex.en: Too much sugar is harmful to your health.
ex.ko: 설탕을 너무 많이 먹으면 건강에 해롭다.

harmony.en: a state in which people or things exist together peacefully or well

ex.en: The two families live in harmony.

harmony.en: (music) the pleasant combination of different musical notes played together

ex.en: The singers created beautiful harmony.

harvest.en: gather a crop from a farm or field

ex.en: Farmers harvest rice in the fall.

harvest.en: the crop gathered, or the season when it is gathered

ex.en: It was a good harvest this year.

hate.en: dislike someone or something very strongly

ex.en: I hate waiting in long lines.
ex.ko: 나는 긴 줄을 서서 기다리는 것을 싫어한다.

hate.en: a very strong feeling of dislike

ex.en: The movie is about a story of love and hate.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 사랑과 증오에 관한 이야기이다.

headphone.en: a device worn over or in the ears to listen to sound privately

ex.en: He listens to music with his headphones on.

headteacher.en: (British English) the teacher in charge of a school

ex.en: The headteacher welcomed the new students.
Heat the soup before serving.

heat.en: the quality of being hot; warmth

ex.en: I can't stand the heat in summer.
ex.ko: 나는 여름의 더위를 참을 수 없다.

heating.en: a system that makes a building warm

ex.en: Turn on the heating, it's cold in here.

heavily.en: to a great degree; a lot

ex.en: It rained heavily all night.
The staff were very helpful.

hero.en: a person admired for great courage or achievements

ex.en: Firefighters are true heroes.

hero.en: the main character in a story, movie, or play

ex.en: The hero of the novel is a young detective.

herself.en: used to refer to a woman or girl as the same person just mentioned

ex.en: She made the dress herself.

hey.cue: 가볍게 부르거나 놀랄 때 쓰는 말
hey.en: used to attract someone's attention or express surprise

ex.en: Hey, wait for me!

high school.en: a school for students usually between the ages of 14 and 18

ex.en: She is a junior in high school.

highway.en: a major road for cars and other vehicles

ex.en: We took the highway to save time.

hike.en: a long walk, especially in the countryside or mountains

ex.en: We went on a hike in the mountains.

hike.en: walk a long way, especially for exercise or pleasure

ex.en: They hiked for six hours to reach the top.
ex.ko: 그들은 정상에 도착하기 위해 여섯 시간 동안 걸었다.

hike.en: a sudden increase in price, cost, or salary

ex.en: There was a sharp hike in gas prices.

hiking.en: the activity of walking long distances, especially in nature

ex.en: Hiking is a popular weekend activity.

himself.en: used to refer to a man or boy as the same person just mentioned

ex.en: He introduced himself to the class.

hip hop.en: a type of popular music that features rap and a strong rhythm

ex.en: She loves dancing to hip hop music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 힙합 음악에 맞춰 춤추는 것을 좋아한다.

hit.en: strike someone or something with force

ex.en: The ball hit the window.

hit.en: the act of one thing hitting another with force

ex.en: The car took a hard hit in the accident.

hit.en: a song, movie, or show that becomes very popular

ex.en: The song became a huge hit last summer.
ex.ko: 그 노래는 지난여름 큰 히트작이 되었다.

hit.en: reach a particular place, level, or amount

ex.en: Sales hit a record high this year.

hockey.en: a team sport played by hitting a ball or puck with a curved stick

ex.en: They watched an ice hockey game last night.

honey.en: a sweet, sticky substance made by bees

ex.en: She added honey to her tea.

honey.en: a word used to address someone you love

ex.en: Honey, dinner is ready.
We honor our teachers for their hard work.
ex.ko: 우리는 선생님들의 노고에 대해 존경을 표한다.

honor.en: the good reputation and respect that someone earns

ex.en: It was a great honor to receive the award.
ex.ko: 그 상을 받은 것은 큰 명예였다.

horror.en: a feeling of great fear or shock

ex.en: She watched in horror as the car crashed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공포에 질려 그 차가 충돌하는 것을 지켜보았다.

host.en: a person who invites and takes care of guests at an event

ex.en: The host greeted every guest at the door.

host.en: a person who leads a TV show, radio program, or event

ex.en: She is the host of a popular cooking show.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인기 있는 요리 프로그램의 진행자이다.

ex.en: The city will host the next Olympics.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 다음 올림픽을 주최할 것이다.

however.en: used to introduce a statement that contrasts with what was just said

ex.en: I wanted to go; however, I was too tired.
However hard he tried, he couldn't solve it.
ex.ko: 그가 아무리 애써도, 그는 그것을 풀 수 없었다.

hug.en: put your arms around someone to show love or comfort

ex.en: She hugged her mother at the airport.

hug.en: the act of putting your arms around someone

ex.en: He gave his son a big hug.

human.en: a person, as opposed to an animal or machine

ex.en: Humans have lived on Earth for thousands of years.
ex.ko: 인간은 수천 년 동안 지구에서 살아왔다.

human.en: relating to or typical of people

ex.en: Everyone makes human mistakes sometimes.
There were about a hundred people at the party.
ex.ko: 파티에는 약 백 명의 사람들이 있었다.

hunter.en: a person who hunts wild animals

ex.en: The hunter tracked the deer through the forest.
He felt too ill to go to work.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 아파서 출근할 수 없었다.

ex.en: It is illegal to park here.

image.en: the general impression that a person or thing gives to others

ex.en: The company wants to improve its public image.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 대중적 이미지를 개선하고 싶어한다.

image.en: a picture, especially one on a screen or in print

ex.en: The website is full of high-quality images.

imagination.cue: 실제가 아닌 것을 마음속에 그리는 능력
imagination.en: the ability to create pictures or ideas in your mind

ex.en: Children have a great imagination.

impact.en: a powerful effect that something has on someone or something else

ex.en: The new law had a big impact on small businesses.
ex.ko: 그 새 법은 소상공인들에게 큰 영향을 미쳤다.

impact.en: have a strong effect on someone or something

ex.en: Climate change impacts every country.
ex.ko: 기후 변화는 모든 나라에 영향을 미친다.

impact.en: the force of one object hitting another

ex.en: The impact of the crash damaged both cars.
ex.ko: 그 충돌의 충격으로 두 차 모두 손상되었다.

impatient.en: unable to wait calmly for something

ex.en: She got impatient waiting in the long line.
Teachers stress the importance of reading every day.
ex.ko: 선생님들은 매일 독서하는 것의 중요성을 강조한다.

importantly.en: used to introduce a more important point

ex.en: More importantly, we need to fix the main problem first.
ex.ko: 더 중요하게도, 우리는 먼저 주요 문제를 해결해야 한다.

impossible.cue: 할 수 없는, 일어날 수 없는
impossible.en: not able to happen, be done, or be true

ex.en: It's impossible to finish this in one day.
ex.ko: 이것을 하루 만에 끝내는 것은 불가능하다.

impress.en: make someone feel admiration or respect

ex.en: Her presentation impressed everyone in the room.
ex.ko: 그녀의 발표는 그 방에 있던 모든 사람에게 깊은 인상을 주었다.

improve.en: become better, or make something better

ex.en: Her English has improved a lot this year.
ex.ko: 그녀의 영어 실력이 올해 많이 향상되었다.

inch.en: a unit of length, about 2.5 centimeters

ex.en: The phone screen is six inches wide.

inch.en: move forward very slowly, a little at a time

ex.en: The car inched forward in the traffic jam.
ex.ko: 그 차는 교통 정체 속에서 조금씩 앞으로 나아갔다.

include.en: have something as a part of a whole

ex.en: The price includes breakfast.
ex.ko: 그 가격에는 아침 식사가 포함되어 있다.

increase.en: become greater in size, amount, or number

ex.en: The population of the city has increased rapidly.

increase.en: a rise in the amount, size, or number of something

ex.en: There was a big increase in sales last month.

indeed.en: used to emphasize that something is true

ex.en: It was, indeed, a wonderful trip.

independence.cue: 다른 것의 통제를 받지 않는 상태
independence.en: freedom from control by another person, group, or country

ex.en: The country gained independence in 1945.
The results indicate a rise in temperature.

indoor.en: located or done inside a building

ex.en: They have an indoor swimming pool.

inexpensive.en: not costing a lot of money

ex.en: We found an inexpensive hotel near the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변 근처에서 저렴한 호텔을 찾았다.

influence.en: have an effect on how someone or something behaves or develops

ex.en: Her parents influenced her decision to become a doctor.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 그녀가 의사가 되기로 한 결정에 영향을 주었다.

influence.en: the power to affect how someone or something develops or behaves

ex.en: Music had a big influence on his life.
ex.ko: 음악은 그의 삶에 큰 영향을 미쳤다.

injure.en: cause physical harm to a person or animal

ex.en: He injured his knee while playing soccer.

inner.en: located inside or closer to the center

ex.en: The inner room was much quieter.

insect.en: a small creature with six legs, such as an ant or a fly

ex.en: There are many insects in the garden.

inspiration.en: a feeling or idea that makes you want to create or do something

ex.en: The mountains were the inspiration for her painting.
ex.ko: 그 산들은 그녀의 그림에 영감을 주었다.

ex.en: The website gives you instant access to news.
ex.ko: 그 웹사이트는 뉴스에 즉각적인 접근을 제공한다.

instant.en: (of food or drink) quick and easy to prepare

ex.en: He made a cup of instant coffee.
ex.ko: 그는 인스턴트 커피 한 잔을 만들었다.

instant.en: a very short period of time; a moment

ex.en: For an instant, she thought about saying no.
I don't have milk, so I'll use water instead.

instrument.en: an object used to produce musical sounds

ex.en: She plays several musical instruments.

instrument.cue: 어떤 일을 하는 데 쓰는 도구
instrument.en: a tool or device used for a particular purpose

ex.en: The doctor used a special instrument to check his ears.
ex.ko: 의사는 그의 귀를 검사하기 위해 특수 기구를 사용했다.

intelligence.en: the ability to learn, understand, and think

ex.en: Dolphins are known for their high intelligence.

intelligence.en: secret information collected about an enemy or rival

ex.en: The agency gathers intelligence on foreign threats.
ex.ko: 그 기관은 해외 위협에 대한 정보를 수집한다.

intelligent.en: having or showing a high ability to learn and understand

ex.en: She is one of the most intelligent students in class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 반에서 가장 똑똑한 학생 중 한 명이다.

interest.en: a feeling of wanting to know or learn about something

ex.en: She has a strong interest in art history.

interest.en: make someone want to know more about something

ex.en: The topic really interests me.
ex.ko: 그 주제는 나에게 정말 흥미를 끈다.

interest.en: extra money paid on money borrowed or saved

ex.en: The bank pays 2% interest on savings accounts.
ex.ko: 그 은행은 저축 계좌에 2%의 이자를 지급한다.

ex.en: She works for an international company.

invade.en: enter another country by force in order to take control of it

ex.en: The army invaded the neighboring country.

invade.en: intrude on someone's privacy or rights

ex.en: Reporters invaded her privacy after the scandal.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들 이후 기자들이 그녀의 사생활을 침해했다.

invent.en: create something completely new that did not exist before

ex.en: Who invented the telephone?

invent.en: make up a false story or excuse

ex.en: He invented an excuse for being late.
ex.ko: 그는 지각한 것에 대해 변명을 지어냈다.

invention.en: something newly created that did not exist before

ex.en: The smartphone is one of the greatest inventions of our time.
ex.ko: 스마트폰은 우리 시대의 가장 위대한 발명품 중 하나이다.

invite.en: ask someone to come to an event or place

ex.en: They invited us to their wedding.

issue.en: officially give out or publish something

ex.en: The government issued a warning about the storm.
ex.ko: 정부는 그 폭풍에 대한 경고를 발표했다.

issue.en: an important topic or problem that people discuss

ex.en: Climate change is a major issue today.

issue.en: one edition of a magazine or newspaper published at a certain time

ex.en: Have you read this month's issue of the magazine?

itself.en: used to refer to a thing as the same thing just mentioned

ex.en: The house itself is small, but the garden is huge.
ex.ko: 그 집 자체는 작지만, 정원은 아주 크다.

jam.en: a sweet food made from fruit and sugar, spread on bread

ex.en: She spread strawberry jam on her toast.

jam.en: a situation in which traffic cannot move

ex.en: We were stuck in a traffic jam for an hour.
ex.ko: 우리는 한 시간 동안 교통 정체에 갇혀 있었다.

jam.en: push something into a small space, or become stuck

ex.en: The printer is jammed again.

jelly.en: a soft, wobbly food or dessert made with gelatin

ex.en: The kids had jelly for dessert.

joke.en: something said to make people laugh

ex.en: He told a funny joke at the party.

joke.en: say things that are meant to be funny, not serious

ex.en: Don't take it seriously, I was just joking.

journey.cue: 한 곳에서 다른 곳으로 가는 여행
journey.en: the act of traveling from one place to another

ex.en: The journey to the city took five hours.
ex.ko: 그 도시까지의 여정은 다섯 시간 걸렸다.

ex.en: Her face lit up with joy.

junior.en: lower in age, rank, or position

ex.en: She is junior to me by two years.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나보다 두 살 어린 후배이다.

junior.en: (American English) a student in the third year of high school or college

ex.en: He is a junior at the local high school.

junk.en: old or useless things that take up space

ex.en: The garage is full of junk.

killer.en: a person or thing that kills

ex.en: Police are still searching for the killer.
ex.ko: 경찰은 여전히 그 살인자를 찾고 있다.

kilo.en: short for kilogram, a metric unit of weight

ex.en: I bought two kilos of apples.

kilogram.en: a metric unit of weight equal to 1000 grams

ex.en: The baby weighed three kilograms at birth.

kilometer.en: a metric unit of distance equal to 1000 meters

ex.en: The station is two kilometers from here.
She kindly offered to help us move.
ex.ko: 그녀는 친절하게도 우리 이사를 도와주겠다고 했다.

ex.en: He is a kindly old man who loves children.

kingdom.en: a country ruled by a king or queen

ex.en: The story is set in an ancient kingdom.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 고대 왕국을 배경으로 한다.

kingdom.en: (biology) the largest group into which living things are classified, such as animal or plant

ex.en: Mushrooms belong to the fungus kingdom.

id.en: a card that shows who you are

ex.en: You need to show your ID to enter the building.
ex.ko: 그 건물에 들어가려면 신분증을 보여줘야 한다.

id card.en: a card that shows who you are

ex.en: Please carry your ID card at all times.
